headlines.title: Stanford AI Index 2026 Released: AI Models Keep Getting Better Despite Predictions of Plateau
headlines.summary: The 2026 Stanford AI Index reveals AI capabilities continue accelerating despite plateau predictions. Key findings: AI adoption (50%+ globally) outpaces PC and internet growth, US-China are neck-and-neck in model performance, AI data centers now draw 29.6 gigawatts (enough to power New York State), and frontier models now exceed 50% on Humanity's Last Exam (up from 8.8% in 2025).

headlines.title: Neuro-Symbolic AI Breakthrough Cuts Energy Use by 100x While Boosting Accuracy
headlines.summary: Researchers developed a neuro-symbolic AI system combining neural networks with symbolic reasoning. In robotics tests using the Tower of Hanoi puzzle, the system achieved 95% success vs 34% for standard systems, trained in 34 minutes vs 1.5 days, and used 99% less training energy and 95% less inference energy. This could address AI's massive energy consumption problem.

headlines.title: Anthropic vs Trump Administration: Constitutional Crisis Over AI Pentagon Contracts
headlines.source: State of AI Newsletter / Financial Times
headlines.summary: The White House ordered federal agencies to phase out Anthropic products within 6 months after the company refused to remove safety guardrails (autonomous weapons ban, domestic surveillance restrictions) from Pentagon contracts. Three cabinet agencies switched to OpenAI. Anthropic sued, and a federal court blocked further punishment. This establishes precedent: US government treats AI vendors as strategic actors whose policy positions can trigger executive retaliation.

headlines.title: Stellantis and Microsoft Announce Major AI Partnership for Automotive
headlines.summary: Stellantis NV (Chrysler, Jeep, Dodge parent) is teaming up with Microsoft to deploy AI software across its vehicles and operations. The companies will co-develop more than 100 AI tools for customer experience, manufacturing, and vehicle systems. This represents one of the largest automotive-AI partnerships to date.

headlines.title: Six Frontier AI Models Released in Four Weeks: Claude Sonnet 4.6, Gemini 3.1 Pro, GPT-5.4
headlines.summary: February-March 2026 saw unprecedented model release velocity. Anthropic's Claude Sonnet 4.6 scored 79.6% on SWE-bench Verified. Google's Gemini 3.1 Pro doubled reasoning performance and scored 77.1% on ARC-AGI-2. OpenAI's GPT-5.4 achieved 75% on OSWorld (beating human average of 72.4%) with native computer-use and 1M-token context. Chinese lab Zhipu AI released GLM-5 trained on Huawei chips without NVIDIA.

headlines.title: Anthropic Revenue Surges from $14B to $30B+ Annualized in Under Two Months
headlines.summary: Anthropic's annualized revenue exploded from $14B in mid-February to $19B by early March, now exceeding $30B. Enterprise customers spending $1M+ annually doubled to over 1,000. The company signed a major compute deal with Google and Broadcom for multi-gigawatt TPU capacity from 2027, part of a $50B US computing infrastructure pledge.

headlines.title: Human Scientists Still Outperform Best AI Agents on Complex Research Tasks
headlines.summary: Despite rapid AI advancement, the Stanford AI Index found AI agents still struggle with reliable multistep scientific workflows. The best AI agents score roughly half as well as human PhD specialists on complex tasks. However, AI mentions in natural sciences papers grew 30x from 2010-2025, with 80,000+ papers in 2025 alone. New science foundation models emerged including AION-1 for astronomy.

top_story.title: Stanford AI Index 2026: AI Accelerating Despite Predictions of Limits
top_story.source: Stanford HAI / MIT Technology Review / IEEE Spectrum
top_story.summary: The 2026 Stanford AI Index report reveals AI development is accelerating, not plateauing. Key findings: AI models now top 50% on Humanity's Last Exam (up from 8.8% in 2025), AI adoption outpaces PC/internet growth with 50%+ global usage, AI data centers consume 29.6 gigawatts (powering all of New York), US-China are neck-and-neck in model performance, and AI investment hit a record $581 billion in 2025. However, AI still struggles with basic tasks like reading analog clocks, and benchmarks are increasingly broken as models quickly exceed their ceilings.
top_story.whyItMatters: This is the definitive annual report on AI progress, showing that despite widespread predictions of hitting limits, AI capabilities continue improving rapidly. The report documents the massive scale of AI infrastructure buildout, the intensifying US-China competition, and the growing gap between AI capabilities and our ability to measure, regulate, and deploy them safely. It also highlights concerning trends: energy consumption, data center concentration risk, and the fact that AI agents still only score half as well as human PhDs on complex tasks despite the hype.
